mnemonics: KITTEN (Kernel Is The Trivial Element Nullspace) - The kernel contains all vectors that transform into the zero vector, the trivial element.
IMAGine the Output (Image is the set of all possible outputs of the transformation).
keyPoints: The kernel (or null space) of a linear transformation T is the set of all vectors v in the domain such that T(v) = 0.
The image (or range) of a linear transformation T is the set of all vectors w in the codomain such that there exists some vector v in the domain with T(v) = w.
The Rank-Nullity Theorem: For a linear transformation T: V -> W, dim(ker(T)) + dim(im(T)) = dim(V).
commonMistakes: Assuming the kernel is always trivial (only contains the zero vector); it can be much larger.
Confusing the image of the transformation with the entire codomain; the image is only a subspace of the codomain.
Forgetting that the zero vector from the domain is always in the kernel, and the zero vector from the codomain is always in the image.
